programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Soaring property prices have put inner-city living out of the reach of many. But not for architect Ben and artist wife Tania, who decide to think laterally and get creative buying a tiny 64 square metre discarded scrap of land in one of North Melbourne's grittier back lanes. Their plans - to build a three storey, three bedroom, two bathroom home with a large garden - all for the not-so-grand price of $600,000. But their slim budget is in total contrast to their expansive ideas. Ben is working on a revolutionary concreting method using a giant robot that can transform boring concrete walls into soaring works of art. And while it's an interesting idea in theory, using their own family home to test it is high stakes indeed! With the family crammed into rental accommodation while Ben finesses his master work, the pressure mounts while the months drag on. Will his plans to transform concrete buildings into habitable architecture works of art ever become a reality?

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: In 2017, Leigh and Richard bought a derelict 17th-century Cornish flour mill to restore and turn into a home. By January 2021, they were still living in a caravan. Now, three years later, Kevin returns.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Jason and Christine are budget sellers who hate agents. They can't understand why you would part with thousands of dollars in commission when you could sell the home yourself. After all, it can't be that hard. Seven months ago they listed their modest Brisbane house on a do-it-yourself sell internet site. To save even more money, they didn't bother to order a sign or any marketing materials. The sole advertising push was down to one single website. It's no great surprise that the house hasn't sold and there's been precious little interest. Can Andrew persuade our budget-conscious couple that agents are worth the commission, or will they insist on sticking with the DIY approach?

In 2009, 120 people died nearby at Kinglake in what's now known as the Black Saturday bushfires, and as a result Bel and John want a home that's fire proof! With John away at work for much of the build, it's up to Bel to project manage the difficult and complex build. But a number of unexpected challenges make the process harder than anticipated, and with their underground sanctuary looking more like an airport terminal than a stylish home, the pressure reaches breaking point!

programme.desc: In Ohio, the ghost of a crazed murderer will stop at nothing to keep the secrets of his crimes buried in the past. In Massachusetts, a man suffering violent and unrelenting hauntings turns to investigators to save him from possession. In Michigan, an investigator hunting the ghost of a violent prisoner makes a mistake that puts him in grave danger.

programme.title: Hotel Paranormal
programme.desc: A romantic getaway becomes a night of terror, shadowy apparitions terrorise a front-desk clerk during the night shift, and a security guard is accosted in a haunted bathroom at a glitzy California hotel.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Big Easy Motors
programme.desc: From a 1957 Chevy Bel Air convertible, to a 1967 Shelby Mustang GT, Charles Handler turns piles of junk into custom cars. He searches all over Louisiana, including the backwoods and the bayou, for classic cars no one wants, invests his own money to fix them up and restore them, and then sells them to the highest bidder. Their cars can sell for upwards of $50,000, so there's a huge profit to be made. But then again, if they put in the work and the car doesn't sell for what they expect it to, they could come home with no profit at all.

programme.title: Road to Riches
programme.desc: There are collectors and then there's Mick. He's filled his large house with Star Wars memorabilia - every square inch of it. The trouble is Mick's super tolerant wife has decided their newborn baby - appropriately named Leia after Princess Leia - needs some room too! Lucas and Mick pick out pieces that will fetch great money - including a full-size Jar Jar Binks model that needs restoring - before putting it all under the hammer at a specialty auction. Lucas hits the country roads to meet farmer George. It's been drought for too long, and farmer George decides he needs to cash up a few old items, and Lucas is the man to get the job done. Some old football caps of George's great grandfather have been sitting around gathering dust, so he decides to let them go - and one of them is a mid 19th century English test cap! Meanwhile, Adrian has invited Lucas in to look at a piece given to him by his father that might be worth a fortune. It's a jacket reputedly worn by Jimi Hendrix!

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, Truck Night goes back to the '90s, featuring a line-up of rigs from the millennium's last decade. An emotional story motivates one driver, and a razor-thin margin separates the two finalists when they face off in the Green Hell.

programme.sub-title: Energy Problems, Millennial Problems, Community Policing Problems
programme.desc: Wyatt tries to make coal miners go solar, move millennials into battleships, and understand community policing.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: My Adventures With Superman
programme.sub-title: Adventures Of A Normal Man Pt. 1
programme.desc: Secret alien Clark and Jimmy start their internship at The Daily Planet and meet fellow intern Lois Lane - who ropes them into investigating stolen military robots threatening Metropolis!

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: Twitch is assigned to the Spawn case. In his research, Twitch confronts the Spawn in the alley as well as the reporter Lisa Wu. Unfortunately his exploits do not go unnoticed.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: After Cog informs Spawn of his shroud's powers, Spawn transforms himself into human form in order to visit Wanda. Twitch, recovering in the hospital, finds his memory foggy. Terry and Richard Sullivan try to expose Wynn's evil CIA dealings.

programme.title: Big Easy Motors
programme.desc: From a 1957 Chevy Bel Air convertible, to a 1967 Shelby Mustang GT, Charles Handler turns piles of junk into custom cars. He searches all over Louisiana, including the backwoods and the bayou, for classic cars no one wants, invests his own money to fix them up and restore them, and then sells them to the highest bidder. Their cars can sell for upwards of $50,000, so there's a huge profit to be made. But then again, if they put in the work and the car doesn't sell for what they expect it to, they could come home with no profit at all.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Below Deck Mediterranean
programme.desc: Captain Sandy embarks on another season in the Mediterranean out of the world famous yachting hub, the French Riviera. Hannah returns as chief stew determined to put her head down, focus on her work and her relationship with Captain Sandy. After enduring heartbreak, Joao returns with a promotion as Bosun hoping to prove to Captain Sandy he can lead a deck team that includes his best friend and partner-in-crime, Colin. Cordon Bleu trained chef Mila takes control of the galley, but immediately falls ill on day one, forcing the crew to rally and help get the boat in shape in time for the first charter. When the first guests arrive and chef Mila, who is a department of one, is still battling illness, Sandy and the crew wonder if she'll be able to perform her most basic duties.

programme.title: Carnivale
programme.desc: Jonesy gives Ben the job of cleaning out the baggage trailer, prompting snickers and stifled laughter from the rousties. Ben finds the trailer sitting dilapidated and forlorn under a gnarled tree and starts going through the dusty contents. Finding an old suitcase, Ben opens it to reveal a carefully wrapped tuxedo and top hat, the same as the man was wearing in a dream. Ben also notices a faded photograph, which he is shocked to see depicts his mother as a young woman on one side and on the back the mysterious letters HS & Flow. Ben pockets the photo and leaves the mystery-filled trailer, only to be accosted by Samson, angry at Ben for not working with the other rousties. When Ben tells of his job at the baggage trailer, Samson reveals that the trailer doesn't exist - it's an old carny gag. When Ben shows Samson the photo to prove he was in the trailer, Samson realises that there's more to the boy than he had thought.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.sub-title: Attack Of The Five Foot Ten Woman
programme.desc: A laid-back Sunday brunch is ruined when Carrie sees Big and Natasha's wedding announcement in the New York Times.

programme.title: New Nurses
programme.desc: The male nursing trial continues and Anna and Erik attempt to keep their relationship secret.

programme.title: Lockerbie: A Search For Truth
programme.desc: After losing his daughter in the bombing of Pan Am Flight 103, Dr Jim Swire begins a quest for truth, searching for why this was allowed to happen and exactly who was responsible.

programme.title: Lockerbie: A Search For Truth
programme.desc: As Jim continues his campaign for truth and justice, two suspects are indicted for the bombing and his focus has to shift to the fight to bring the men to trial.

programme.title: The Last of Us
programme.desc: While attempting to evade the rebels, Joel and Ellie cross paths with the most wanted man in Kansas City. Kathleen continues her hunt.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The ability to find justice for John Curtis is put in jeopardy when he is freed on bail, the accuser is killed, and the team uncover a possible conspiracy to frame him.

programme.desc: A young woman is strangled, and Yee connects the modus operandi to a potential serial offender, leading Riley to revisit a case that went cold on his watch; Price makes a risky move to bait one of their own on the stand.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team are called in when a couple of kids stumble upon the body of a missing Marine in a frozen lake. The team then has to work to uncover the Marine's connection with a deadly street gang.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a Pentagon cryptographer is found dead, the NCIS team has to discover whether the person was or wasn't a Pentagon mole, and if the person committed suicide or was murdered.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: For this grand tour Paul's journey begins at Loch Hourn on the mainland, takes him over the sea to the to Skye, then heads to the tiny island of Soay before ending on Canna. Along the way, Paul visits the house which was the inspiration for Skyfall, home of Ian Fleming author of Britain's most famous spy, James Bond, embarks on a nerve wracking caving expedition under the summit of Blaven, and discovers the remarkable story of shark fishing off Soay.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: For this final grand tour Paul is heading as far north as you can go in Britain, to the islands of Fetlar, Unst and Yell. These islands, which lie on Scotland's northern frontier, are as much Scandinavian as Scottish and were in fact once part of Norway. Along the way he will sing with the Shanty Yell Men who came together to celebrate their Norse Heritage, learn how to 'roo Shetland sheep the traditional way, and get an opportunity to see up close the wild otters who make their home here in Shetland's pristine waters. And finally, Paul will fulfil a long held ambition to visit Out Stack, the rocky outcrop that is famous for being the 'full stop' at the edge of Britain.

programme.title: A Tale Of Two Thieves
programme.desc: An investigator of the 1963 train robbery in England discusses the heist.

programme.title: The Jinx: Life & Deaths Of Robert Durst
programme.sub-title: The State Of Texas vs. Robert Durst
programme.desc: Galveston, Texas becomes a media circus as the press descends on the sleepy seaside town to cover the trial of Robert Durst for the murder of his neighbour, Morris Black. Robert's multimillion-dollar legal team decides to make an unusual plea, he will admit to having dismembered the corpse, but for the murder will plead not guilty by reason of self-defence and accident. The town of Galveston and the entire country become engaged in the drama of the trial as his lawyers take the unusual step of putting Robert himself on the witness stand.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Broadcasters Mark Ellen and Robert Elms discuss the impact of the first four Roxy Music albums. The popular British band were influential through the 70s and 80s.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: An in-depth look back at some of Peter Gabriel's most iconic music videos, including his 1986 hit Sledgehammer, which won a record nine MTV Video Music Awards and was a hit amongst his fans.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: Many followed the meteoric rise of this young man who was mercurial with an extreme personality. It only took seven films to make John Belushi unforgettable.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael revisits his journey through the Himalaya, travelling through Pakistan, India and Nepal, meeting the Dalai Lama along the way.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From Tirano in Italy, this route spirals 1km up the Alps, past glaciers and through mountains, ending at the base of the majestic Matterhorn.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The episode tracks the launch of Virgin Atlantic and Branson's efforts to attract media attention to his brand, including an attempt to break the transatlantic speed record in a boat, and his balloon adventure across the Atlantic in 1986.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns about a Scottish hero, visits a Highland Games, and discovers how an impressive piece of Victorian engineering ended in tragedy.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo learns how Queen Victoria used to hide from her subjects, discovers how factory workers went deaf, and goes out with a bang in Aberdeenshire.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: Star Wars
programme.sub-title: Attack Of The Clones/Revenge Of The Sith
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Michael Keaton was born September 5, 1951, and is an American actor, best known for playing the DC Comics superhero Bruce Wayne/Batman.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: What happened to Bobbie Gentry? Did the Fyre Festival ruin Ja Rule's career? Why is Phil Collins' music sinister?

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Did the Scorpions end the Cold War? What is Wu-Tang's multi-million-dollar treasure? Kylie Jenner vs Kylie Minogue.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Royal Ascot Royal Enclosure Car Park
programme.desc: Artists are charged with capturing the glamorous racegoers at the Royal Enclosure Picnic.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Watching Portrait Artist of the Year has inspired many a viewer to take up painting and the celebrity sitters often express a desire to have a go themselves. In this year's celebrity special of Portrait Artist of the Year 6 new celebrity artists are invited to take up the challenge. Actors Mark Gatiss, Fenella Woolgar and James Fleet are joined by presenters Anneke Rice and Sophie Morgan, and comedian Jenny Eclair.

programme.title: Gabon: Earth's Last Chance
programme.desc: This enviro-thriller documentary feature tells the extraordinary story of scientist Professor Lee White from Manchester, who ended up becoming the Environment Minister of Gabon in central Africa.

programme.title: Antarctica: A Year On Ice
programme.desc: A visually stunning chronicle of what it is like to live in Antarctica for a full year, including winters isolated from the rest of the world, and enduring months of darkness in the coldest place on Earth.

programme.title: Most Expensive Christmas Cruise Ship
programme.desc: The World's Most Expensive Cruise offers exclusive access and behind-the-scenes insight aboard the most luxurious ship ever built.

programme.title: The Cevennes
programme.desc: Hidden Beauty in the South of France. Imposing plateaus, karst landscapes, deep gorges and vast forests. In the south of France, the Cevennes stretch across the departments of Lozere, Gard and Herault. In 1970, the Cevennes National Park was established, one of the largest national parks in France. In 2011, UNESCO declared the region a World Heritage Site of Humanity. Over the course of almost a year, this documentary goes on a journey of discovery into a unique landscape and its inhabitants, including rare flora and fauna.

In this year's celebrity special of Portrait Artist of the Year 6 new celebrity artists are invited to take up the challenge. Actors Mark Gatiss, Fenella Woolgar and James Fleet are joined by presenters Anneke Rice and Sophie Morgan, and comedian Jenny Eclair.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns about a Scottish hero, visits a Highland Games, and discovers how an impressive piece of Victorian engineering ended in tragedy.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo learns how Queen Victoria used to hide from her subjects, discovers how factory workers went deaf, and goes out with a bang in Aberdeenshire.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: What happened to Bobbie Gentry? Did the Fyre Festival ruin Ja Rule's career? Why is Phil Collins' music sinister?

programme.sub-title: Attack Of The Clones/Revenge Of The Sith
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Michael Keaton was born September 5, 1951, and is an American actor, best known for playing the DC Comics superhero Bruce Wayne/Batman.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo rides one of Scotland's most picturesque railways, visits Scotland's smallest station, and learns what went into a Victorian-style spa break.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In the late 1980s Fox was desperate to be a major network - and flailing. Industry titan James L. Brooks, television veteran Sam Simon, and crude comic strip cartoonist Matt Groening combined to create a series of shorts for The Tracey Ullman Show that would propel Fox and its creators into television stardom, Cowabunga, man!

programme.title: The Prince and the Paedophile
programme.desc: The questionable friendship between Prince Andrew and millionaire paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. An investigation into the British connections to this tawdry tale.

programme.title: The Royals Revealed
programme.sub-title: Harry And Edward: Princes In Exile
programme.desc: A journey through all facets of royal life past and present, in all its grandeur, splendour and controversy.

programme.title: Questioning Darwin
programme.desc: Three months after graduating from Cambridge, Darwin was offered the chance to sail around the world on the HMS Beagle. The five-year journey was a dream for Darwin, who had a great passion for nature. Yet on the trip, Darwin saw his convictions of a divine world order shattered. It turns out that Darwin was genuinely torn between the religious beliefs he espoused as a young man, and the evidence he collected that would challenge the idea of Creationism.

programme.title: Star Hotel Riot
programme.desc: The Star Hotel riot, occasioned by the closing of a popular pub, was one of the largest riots in Australian history. An estimated 4,000 people fought with police on the streets of Newcastle, New South Wales on the night of 19 September 1979.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Problems plagued the launch of The Simpsons, causing it to miss its original debut date and throwing the future of the entire series into question. When the pilot finally aired in December of 1989, it wasn't the first episode produced, but a last-minute replacement entitled "Simpsons Roasting on an Open Fire." What happened next was a Christmas miracle that resonates to this day.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan presents the semi final of Landscape Artist of the Year at the Thames Barrier in East London. programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons writer's room is the stuff of TV legend. It would churn out hilarious scripts like a Ford automobile assembly line, turning what could have been a niche, off-beat miss into a surprise hit. The phenomenal success of the show has almost always been attributed to the irreverent and groundbreaking writing, but while the writers toiled away, tensions rose among the three creators.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Nicolas Kim Coppola was born January 7, 1964 and known professionally as Nicolas Cage. He is best known for his performance in The Rock (1996), Con Air (1997) and City of Angels (1998).

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Did Napster destroy the album, but ultimately save the music industry? Who really killed Keith Moon's chauffeur? Who's responsible for Who Let the Dogs Out?

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Achy Breaky, Wardrobe Malfunction And Satan
programme.desc: What is the true story behind Billy Ray Cyrus' smash hit Achy Breaky Heart? Who is responsible for Janet Jackson's wardrobe malfunction? Did Led Zeppelin really worship Satan?

programme.desc: The grand finale has arrived. Just three artists have made it through to the last round of the competition, where they're charged with capturing the weird and fantastical tourist village of Portmeirion.

programme.desc: Kathleen Soriano, Tai Shan Schierenberg and Kate Bryan judge more portraits in the heats.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: Cricket thrives on partnerships, showcasing how two players can achieve greater success together than alone.

programme.title: From The Ashes
programme.desc: The uplifting and remarkable tale of a summer that changed England forever. July 1981, rioting and discontent rule the streets of Britain. Meanwhile, on the playing fields, England are 1-0 down after two tests against the old enemy, Australia; and Ian Botham, their iconic all-rounder, has resigned as English captain, moments before being pushed.

programme.title: G.O.A.T.s with Photographer Walter Iooss
programme.desc: Celebrate the greatest athletes of all time through the extraordinary work of America's most iconic sports photographer, Walter Iooss.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael gets up close with a piece of natural history, visits a garden used as a viewing platform for public hangings, and experiences a timepiece like no other.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo takes to the sea with the heroes of the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, visits a stormy coastal railway, and has a close personal encounter with his boyhood hero.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Just as critical to the success of the show as the writers, the animators had drama and tension of their own. The funky, punk rock animation house Klasky Csupo, led by Gabor Csupo had secured the contract of a lifetime, but could they hold on to The Simpsons? Through internal strife and natural disasters, the animators gave The Simpsons their signature look.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Jeremy Irons was born 19 September 1948 and is an English actor and activist. His first major film role came in the romantic drama The French Lieutenant's Woman (1981), for which he received a BAFTA nomination for Best Actor.

programme.title: History's Greatest Heists
programme.desc: Since the creation of currency, money has made the world go round and people have done anything and everything in their power to get their hands on a lot of it, including formulating some of the most devious and high-stakes heist attempts of all time. Using dramatic recreations, dynamic storytelling and cutting-edge visual effects, alongside first-person witness accounts from the people who were there, the series delves into the intricate schemes and audacity of the criminal masterminds who risked their freedom for a shot at a lifetime of wealth and riches. Brosnan, who is embedded into each heist through state-of-the-art technology, brings each global news headline to life by putting viewers at the heart of the action and breaking down every aspect of the plan including the conniving team, the mark, the execution and finally the aftermath.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: We follow the winner's progress as they head to Holland to research their 10,000 pound commission for the Queen's House at Royal Museums Greenwich.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Celeste, Lydia West, Alastair Campbell: The competition continues as the artists create portraits and present them to the judges.

programme.title: Elon Musk's Twitter Takeover
programme.desc: The story of Elon Musk's long and often troubled relationship with Twitter, charting his journey from one of the site's most provocative users to its sole proprietor.

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey: What's Occurred
programme.desc: An uplifting and touching documentary special which tells the story of one of Britain's best loved sitcoms as it comes to an end after 17 years.

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

The funky, punk rock animation house Klasky Csupo, led by Gabor Csupo had secured the contract of a lifetime, but could they hold on to The Simpsons? Through internal strife and natural disasters, the animators gave The Simpsons their signature look.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Jeremy Irons was born 19 September 1948 and is an English actor and activist. His first major film role came in the romantic drama The French Lieutenant's Woman (1981), for which he received a BAFTA nomination for Best Actor.

programme.title: Europe's Waterways
programme.sub-title: Holland And Belgium - From Rotterdam To The Belgian Ardennes
programme.desc: Venturing upstream on Europe's waterways, the series discovers five regions of the continent from an unknown perspective and visits people who live from, on and with water. Rivers, canals, lake regions: they have shaped Europe's cultures for centuries - they are a place to live, transport arteries and sources of energy. Five journeys on watercourses broad and narrow lead to Italy, Germany, France, Poland, Holland and Belgium. Along the way: new projects for the future of water in Europe.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: The creative minds behind some of George Michael's most memorable music videos reveal how they brought his hits to life on screen.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: The story of how a little-known singer-songwriter from Paddington named Declan McManus became the world-famous Elvis Costello through his powerful introspective lyrics and innovative music videos.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: On August 9, 1969, 26-year-old Sharon and her unborn child are stabbed to death 16 times by a Satanist sect led by its self-appointed guru, Charles Manson.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: Tenerife - In The Realm Of Fire
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A luxurious 500 mile African train safari where we'll be travelling on Rovos Rail's vintage hotel train. Lovingly re-built by the Rovos family to the highest of specifications, this beautiful 14 carriage train leaves Pretoria for its three-day journey to Durban on the coast.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: Cricket thrives on partnerships, showcasing how two players can achieve greater success together than alone.

programme.title: Paul Kelly: Stories of me
programme.desc: This film charts the remarkable life of one of Australia's most gifted and beloved singer-songwriters. For almost 40 years, in over 350 songs, Paul Kelly has been mapping out the Australian landscape and its people. For the first time in a feature length film, the man behind the music is revealed, as Kelly speaks candidly about the people who have helped shape his life and music. Featuring rare archival footage, never-before-seen live performances, interviews with Kelly's family members, former and current band members, music contemporaries and many more.

programme.title: Bernadette Peters: Live In Australia
programme.desc: Broadway star Bernadette Peters headlines the 2009 Adelaide Cabaret Festival. From her opening number, Let Me Entertain You, Peters brings wit, emotion and her inimitable style to a range of musical numbers and pop classics. Highlights include a rendition of Fever from atop a grand piano, a heart-rending Rose's Turn (from Gypsy) and a performance of her self-penned charity single Kramer's Song.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The episode chronicles Virgin Atlantic's battle with British Airways, ending in a libel suit win for Branson. From outsider to wealthy capitalist, Branson continues to push for world records and promotional attention for his company, setting off on a nail-biting Pacific Ocean balloon voyage. He announces plans to develop a spaceflight company to take commercial passengers into space, beginning a decade long pursuit.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Beginning with the Eurythmics' Sweet Dreams and finishing with Walking on Broken Glass, we see the different approach Annie takes with each video.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A look at the making of the iconic music videos of Paul Weller, the 'Modfather', whose solo hits include The Changingman and You Do Something to Me.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: In November 1981, at the age of 43, Natalie Wood drowns in a tragic accident - a death in which the circumstances have never been clarified.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael revisits Tibet and Yunnan in China, travelling through eastern India arriving in the Himalayan Kingdom of Bhutan before ending the series in Bangladesh cruising down the Ganges into the Bay of Bengal.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The Chihuahua Pacific Railway, known as the EL Chepe in northern Mexico, took a hundred years to build. From Creel to the coast, it travels through the spectacular Copper Canyon region.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan plans to use an American actor in a German propaganda film to get information home.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan steals the Luftwaffe codebook, but can't use it when Newkirk accidentally drops it down a well.

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz
programme.desc: Alcatraz is the most secure prison of its time. It is believed that no one can ever escape from it, until three daring men make a possibly successful attempt at escaping from one of the most infamous prisons in the world.

programme.title: Sabrina
programme.desc: A charming and humorous romance, aglow with some of Hollywood's brightest lights. Audrey Hepburn, Humphrey Bogart and William Holden star in a Cinderella story directed by the legendary Billy Wilder.

programme.title: The Brady Bunch
programme.desc: Marcia, Jan and Peter each get jobs at the local ice cream parlour, Marcia loves her new job until the shiftless Peter fouls things up and Jan outperforms her.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Arson is intended to remove the rodeo doctor and his patient permanently.

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Two critics take opposite views of a play based on one of Jessica's novels, Mainly Murder. Then, one is suspected of the murder of the other.

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Miscommunication deteriorates into a family feud when Margo's windbreak puts the Goods' fruits in the shade.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber attacks another prisoner after receiving a Dear John letter from his girlfriend, Denise. Meanwhile, Fletch starts questioning his daughter, Ingrid, over her personal life.

programme.title: The Goodies
programme.desc: When the United States Army asks the Goodies to get rid of a large container, with the words Tomato soup on its side, they take it out to sea in an attempt to ditch it.

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Business is keeping Richard away from the estate. When Audrey hears of an attempted takeover coup by some of his directors, she calls on the help of her influential uncle.

programme.title: To Catch a Thief
programme.desc: When a reformed jewel thief is suspected of returning to his former occupation, he must ferret out the real thief in order to prove his innocence.

programme.title: Marnie
programme.desc: A woman's complex life leads her into becoming a compulsive thief, while her husband tries not only to save her from jail but salvage their marriage.

programme.title: Mutiny on the Bounty
programme.desc: An 18th-century British naval vessel sets off for Tahiti but as the voyage progresses, tensions mount between the heartless captain and his conscientious chief officer.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Beau seeks refuge in a cave that bandits use as a hideout.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: Hymie enters the Olympics after Kaos succeeds in preventing US athletes from competing.

programme.title: Get Smart
programme.desc: When a Kaos agent impersonating the president calls Control on their hotline, Control is sent on a wild goose chase.

Teleplay was written by Sam Locke and Joel Rapp. Directed by Hollingsworth Morse.

programme.title: Rawhide
programme.desc: A dying woman stumbles into the cattle drive. Her husband, a feared gunslinger turned rancher, soon shows up and accuses the drovers of shooting her. programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil
programme.desc: Wally and Dave run a newsstand in New York. Wally, loud-mouthed and cocky, is blind, while quiet, polite Dave is deaf. When a man is murdered outside their newsstand, they are the only witnesses: Dave saw someone's legs and Wally smelled her Shalimar perfume. But the police think they've collared their first deaf and blind murderers. Forced to go on the run to clear their names, Wally and Dave somehow manage to outrun and outwit the entire New York City police force and a mysterious band of ruthless criminals, including a beautiful, enigmatic killer and her ice-cold partner.

programme.desc: Pretty In Pink's Howard Deutch directs John Candy and Dan Aykroyd in this comedy about a father who plans a relaxing family vacation in the woods, but his obnoxious brother-in-law and his maladjusted brood blast in to spend the week with them, resulting in some hilarious hijinks - especially when a very large bear decides to pay a visit. Writer John Hughes also penned The Breakfast Club, Ferris Bueller's Day Off and Uncle Buck. Oscar nominated actress Annette Bening (American Beauty) makes her film debut as Dan Aykroyd's cranky wife.

programme.desc: Moreno copes with her upcoming custody battle for Melissa as she hunts for a carjacker responsible for shooting two children left in the back seat.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off Wedding Special
programme.desc: This is the story of the Great British wedding cake - the origins of the most important cake in our lives. The three finalists from last year's "Great British Bake Off" hope to prove that they can master it.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom transforms his favourite Chinese dishes into a delicious Sunday feast. First up, he makes aromatic crispy duck served with pancakes and homemade plum sauce.

programme.title: Michel Roux's French Country Cooking
programme.desc: Top chef Michel Roux cooks four of his favourite dishes while holidaying in the South of France. He'll be sourcing ingredients from local producers, from a goat farmer in the Cevennes mountains to an organic vegetable grower in the Ardeche. For Michel, cooking on holiday is about keeping things simple. His delicious goats cheese tart and fish with fresh herbs are a feast for the eyes, but will his classic Diplomat Pudding please his hardest critic, his wife Giselle?

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: Rick Stein heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik. A perfect winter break enriched with a smorgasbord of the best Iceland has to offer.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James' travels have provided him with lots of culinary inspiration. He makes a boozy pudding and a fantastic recipe he netted on a recent fishing trip. James is joined by special guest Antonio Carluccio.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen and they pay a visit to the Isle of Man during the famous TT race warm up week. James serves up proper biker food- pork burgers with a spicy apple sauce, while Lisa visits some local organic juice producers.

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: A small, elegant bistro in France has inspired Raymond for this theme: Le Petit Plats. He promises small plates bursting with fresh ingredients and packed with flavour.

programme.title: River Cottage to the Core
programme.desc: With all the incredible fruit that is growing in Britain today, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall wants to open our eyes to the endless and mouth-watering possibilities at our fingertips. Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall wants to rip up the cookery rule book to show just how deliciously fruit can enhance and transform meat, fish and pudding dishes, proving it shouldn't be restricted to crumbles, chutneys and pies. We'll follow Hugh as he offers his unique take on everything, from savoury strawberries to fruity pizzas. He'll point the finger at the supermarkets' quest for cosmetic perfection and shows us how we can grow our own fruit at home that will always taste fantastic, regardless of how it looks. Hugh kicks off the series with the much-anticipated first fruits of the season. To prove that strawberries can be served as a savoury, he's staging a surprising cooking demonstration with strawberry steak and salad recipes, but will his guests buy into it? He also throws a big party to celebrate the reopening of his barn, a year-and-a-half after it was completely destroyed by fire. He also takes to the sea, the hedgerow and the kitchen garden in search of inspiration for the menu. There'll be crackling-covered, fruit-stuffed loins of roast pork, a delicious lemon tart, and aromatic mojitos using unlikely foraged ingredients, including blackberry leaves and lemon balm.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Specials
programme.desc: The Food CIA team explore health-boosting science: vegan bacon, sleep-friendly lettuce, sun-protecting foods, sweet potatoes, fibre, and the secret to staying youthful with black garlic.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace receives a load of corn fresh off the boat from Argentina and follows its journey through the largest breakfast cereal factory in Europe as it is cooked, milled and flavoured to become Crunchy Nut Cornflakes.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.title: The Great Kiwi Bake Off
programme.desc: This season brings a new group of 10 baking hopefuls to the tent to impress the returning judges Sue Fleischl and Dean Brettschneider.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off Wedding Special
programme.desc: This is the story of the Great British wedding cake - the origins of the most important cake in our lives. The three finalists from last year's "Great British Bake Off" hope to prove that they can master it.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace receives a load of corn fresh off the boat from Argentina and follows its journey through the largest breakfast cereal factory in Europe as it is cooked, milled and flavoured to become Crunchy Nut Cornflakes.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Specials
programme.desc: The Food CIA team explore health-boosting science: vegan bacon, sleep-friendly lettuce, sun-protecting foods, sweet potatoes, fibre, and the secret to staying youthful with black garlic.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul's spending his first week in Japan's mega city capital, Tokyo. He has a lesson in Japanese restaurant etiquette, sees Godzilla and visits one of the worlds cheapest Michelin starred restaurants. He sniffs out an extraordinary bakery which puts a very big smile on his face, then samples the Japanese practice of solo dining and the even weirder practice of solo Karaoke. Finally, Paul heads off on a karting tour of the city with a man called Lady Beard. After fishing for their lunch, they cuddle some puppies, risk life and limb on the streets of Tokyo and finish Paul's first week with a three-course dinner up the posh end of a jumbo jet well, sort of.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This couple love their brands, their labels and eating out regularly. Money hasn't always been an issue, but Covid forced a change in their circumstances and they're desperate to save money, reduce their waste, cut back on salt, repurpose their leftovers and learn to use their oven! Chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj have their work cut out for them this week, but the couple want to change, they just need some help to move them away from the snack isle and into their own beautiful kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon's Ultimate Home Cooking course continues with a flavour-packed Middle Eastern breakfast, lunch and dinner. Recipes include a delicious twist on classic hummous made with beetroot and wonderful lamb koftas full of herbs and spices.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: Festive Specials
programme.desc: Paul Hollywood, Prue Leith, Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding see in the New Year with 2018 Bake Off runner-up Kim-Joy and fellow baker Jon, and Hermine and Rowan from the 2020 Bake Off bubble.

programme.title: MasterChef UK
programme.desc: Tonight, they face just one intense challenge. They must cook two courses that will excite not just John and Gregg, but also some of MasterChefs most inspiring winners, finalists and contestants, who have all gone on to careers in the food industry.

programme.title: Taste of the Country
programme.desc: It's the start of summer and the adventure begins for Julius Roberts on his Dorset smallholding with incredible, irresistible and easy cooking and swimming at the nearby Jurassic Coast.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits the biggest tortilla factory in Europe. The Coventry site covers more than 21,000 square metres, and makes 60,000 tonnes of snacks every year.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: This week we're down to 11 bakers who must rise to the challenge in bread week, and take on an afternoon tea classic with pumpkin scones.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Specials
programme.desc: The Food CIA team explore health-boosting science: vegan bacon, sleep-friendly lettuce, sun-protecting foods, sweet potatoes, fibre, and the secret to staying youthful with black garlic.

programme.title: River Cottage to the Core
programme.desc: With all the incredible fruit that is growing in Britain today, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall wants to open our eyes to the endless and mouth-watering possibilities at our fingertips. Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall wants to rip up the cookery rule book to show just how deliciously fruit can enhance and transform meat, fish and pudding dishes, proving it shouldn't be restricted to crumbles, chutneys and pies. We'll follow Hugh as he offers his unique take on everything, from savoury strawberries to fruity pizzas. He'll point the finger at the supermarkets' quest for cosmetic perfection and shows us how we can grow our own fruit at home that will always taste fantastic, regardless of how it looks. Hugh kicks off the series with the much-anticipated first fruits of the season. To prove that strawberries can be served as a savoury, he's staging a surprising cooking demonstration with strawberry steak and salad recipes, but will his guests buy into it? He also throws a big party to celebrate the reopening of his barn, a year-and-a-half after it was completely destroyed by fire. He also takes to the sea, the hedgerow and the kitchen garden in search of inspiration for the menu. There'll be crackling-covered, fruit-stuffed loins of roast pork, a delicious lemon tart, and aromatic mojitos using unlikely foraged ingredients, including blackberry leaves and lemon balm.

programme.title: Masterchef UK: Celebrity
programme.desc: The three remaining celebrities must cook a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John and Gregg, but also three Celebrity MasterChef champions.

programme.desc: Molly digs deep for her latest commission as her Gran needs a cake to celebrate older Older Peoples' Day. Her stunning 2 ft square allotment themed cake comes complete with a cake and biscuit tool shed, florist paste picket fence, fondant crazy paving, and dozens of sugar paste details including fruit and veg, and even a pair of edible muddy boots. Former fashion designer turned cake artist Nastassja, must make a birthday cake as glamorous as the birthday girl herself, to take centre stage at a dinner party. Made from amaretto and red velvet cake covered in buttercream and gold leaf, her toughest challenge will be adding over 200 wafer paper discs and getting this 2 and a half foot tall cake to hang upside down.

programme.desc: In London, cake maker Nastassja's pushing the boat out for her latest commission to celebrate little Noah's first birthday. Asked for a Noah's Ark themed cake, Nastassja's planning to position her revolving edible boat, filled with keep sake toy animals on a giant 2 and a half foot tall spurt of water made from 12 layers of vanilla sponge, topped with rice cereal and marshmallow waves. Molly's hoping to create a bit of flutter, as she creates a giant Butterfuly Cake for a birthday surprise. Sculpted from vanilla sponge sandwiched with white chocolate and raspberry buttercream, she'll use all her sugar craft and air brushing skills to capture the insect's delicate and distinctive blue and pink markings in vibrant sugar paste.

programme.desc: It's Food Unwrapped's Healthy New Year. This special episode is stuffed with virtuous food hacks and little-known science secrets to help turbo-charge your health and well-being. Jimmy discovers if chia seeds could be the key to curbing overeating. Briony asks why producers of gluten free products are seemingly charging more for less. Matt lets the dogs out and discover whether raw pet food could be the key to a healthy pooch. And Kate discovers some surprising science about the watermelon's superpowers.

Sculpted to look like it's bursting out of the water, Ben struggles with its complex shape. Baking meets building for Lancashire based Molly, as she sculpts a cake replica of Queen Anne's Summer House to celebrate a construction company's 40th anniversary. programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals

programme.title: Mary Berry's Love To Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry loves to explore new ideas that inspire her to make stand-out dishes that really tantalise the tastebuds.

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
programme.desc: Vikkie and Wayne in London have a bad habit of cooking a nightly back-up meal for their fussy kids. It's making their shopping bills double the national average and causing their food waste to sky-rocket.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood: A Baker's Life
programme.desc: Paul looks back at his early career, from an apprentice baker to working at a five-star stately home. On the menu: fresh fruit doughnuts, pork pie and Danish pastries.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less
programme.desc: Chris and Gregg meet the Hoyland family. Dad Derek, who is coeliac, hasn't eaten the same meal as his family for nearly six years. Mum, Clair, is brand-obsessed, filling the trolley with frozen convenience food.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul is now heading out to travel more widely across Japan by road, rail and air. On the road, he happens across a world record breaking bakery. On the railway, he lives out his ultimate train spotter fantasy rides the Shinkansen or bullet train as we know it and experiences the best railway buffet food on earth. And a short flight takes him to the sub tropical islands of Okinawa, once celebrated as the healthiest place in the world, but now becoming addicted to American style fast food. Paul also visits two of Japan's best known cities. First he visits Japan's ancient capital Kyoto where, as well as trying his hand at being a Ninja, meat lover Paul attempts to enjoy tofu in its many forms. Then he moves on to Hiroshima. Whilst most of the world only knows this city as the place they dropped the first atomic bomb, these days the city has a growing reputation across Japan for its food. Paul visits possibly the smallest restaurant in Hiroshima to meet its owner, a bomb survivor who continues to cook and serve the city's signature dish. In what Paul fondly remembers as one of his happiest and favourite days in Japan, she teaches Paul to cook the famous Hiroshima-style Okonimayaki. Finally, Paul travels way up north into the snow to take part in a particularly brutal rice festival. It's a contest where the north and south of the town fight it out with 6 metre long bamboo sticks to see whether they'll get a good rice harvest. Paul gets stuck into to both the fighting and the sake!

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon concludes with an episode showcasing home cooked food that's incredibly easy to cook but sensational enough to serve for any special occasion. Gordon's son Jack helps him cook an unforgettable dinner of beef fillet with salsa verde.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Love To Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry loves to explore new ideas that inspire her to make stand-out dishes that really tantalise the tastebuds.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This week we have two proudly single mums who've joined forces to rise their kids, start businesses and save money but somehow, they're not saving and family meals end up being the quickest, and most processed, fix available. Enter chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj who are going help take the stress - and sugar - out of the family meals and truly help them join forces to stay on budget.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul is now heading out to travel more widely across Japan by road, rail and air. On the road, he happens across a world record breaking bakery. On the railway, he lives out his ultimate train spotter fantasy rides the Shinkansen or bullet train as we know it and experiences the best railway buffet food on earth. And a short flight takes him to the sub tropical islands of Okinawa, once celebrated as the healthiest place in the world, but now becoming addicted to American style fast food. Paul also visits two of Japan's best known cities. First he visits Japan's ancient capital Kyoto where, as well as trying his hand at being a Ninja, meat lover Paul attempts to enjoy tofu in its many forms. Then he moves on to Hiroshima. Whilst most of the world only knows this city as the place they dropped the first atomic bomb, these days the city has a growing reputation across Japan for its food. Paul visits possibly the smallest restaurant in Hiroshima to meet its owner, a bomb survivor who continues to cook and serve the city's signature dish. In what Paul fondly remembers as one of his happiest and favourite days in Japan, she teaches Paul to cook the famous Hiroshima-style Okonimayaki. Finally, Paul travels way up north into the snow to take part in a particularly brutal rice festival. It's a contest where the north and south of the town fight it out with 6 metre long bamboo sticks to see whether they'll get a good rice harvest. Paul gets stuck into to both the fighting and the sake!

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Nastassja attempts an edible baby's mobile suspended 4 and a half feet off the ground for a little boy's first birthday. Sculpted from chocolate brownie cake and rice cereal and marshmallow mix, this cloud themed hanging cake has cloud and giraffe shaped cookies dangling from it. Rosie's also facing a first birthday challenge as she makes a cake replica of a beloved pet to celebrate TV presenter Lorraine Kelly's dog Angus's special day.

programme.title: Chopped Next Gen
programme.desc: Four young, hip and hungry chefs face scorpion powder in round one.

programme.title: MasterChef UK
programme.desc: Tonight, they face just one intense challenge. They must cook two courses that will excite not just John and Gregg, but also some of MasterChef's most inspiring winners, finalists and contestants, who have all gone on to careers in the food industry.

programme.title: Taste of the Country
programme.desc: Julius is taking us on an emotional and fun journey as he heads into the woods beside the farmhouse to forage for cherries just like his beloved Granny showed him when he was a small boy.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers must fulfil Matt's mantra that patisserie is perfection by piling pastries on the judges' plates.

programme.title: The Great Canadian Bake Off
programme.desc: This program welcomes 10 of the country's most passionate bakers to the iconic white baking tent to face Cake Week, and demonstrate talent and skill in the first of many challenges.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This week's family are American expats excited to rise their kids the 'Kiwi way' but first, they need to understand how seasonal produce works in New Zealand and with several serious health issues they also need a little help to eat their way to wellness, which is where chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj come in!

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four of Wales' best chefs compete to stay in the competition over canapes, starters and fish dishes celebrating British animation and illustration for Paddington's 65th birthday.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Love To Cook
programme.desc: 'The more the merrier' is a motto Mary Berry lives by. What is better than cooking crowd-pleasing delights and meeting others who also adore feeding the masses?

programme.title: Gino's Italy: Secrets of the South
programme.desc: Gino arrives in Matera James Bond style and explores the cave town where he samples his father's favourite drink Amaro Lucano and Nun's Breast cakes.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Fantastic Feast
programme.desc: Dame Mary Berry knows a thing or two about cooking up a Fantastic Feast, so she's sharing her cooking skills with 3 novice cooks - fireman Mark, youth worker Callum and boxing instructor Thaer who want to throw a spectacular afternoon tea to say thank you to Soraya, who runs a local youth charity. With the help of Dame Mary, can they pull it off and give Soraya a thank you surprise she'll never forget?

programme.title: MasterChef UK
programme.desc: It's the third week of MasterChef and coming up to the halfway point of the Heats. This year the judges are mixing it up, introducing a whole new set of challenges that the passionate amateurs must complete to progress in the competition.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a Manchester factory that churns out 6 million Jaffa Cakes every single day. Cherry Healey is in Jaffa, the city responsible for growing the fruit that gives these cakes their name.

programme.desc: The bakers get busy with batter, beating up wild waffles, an apple clafoutis and a 60-layer crepe cake.

programme.title: Masterchef UK: Celebrity
programme.desc: The first challenge for the five hopefuls is the brand new Under the Cloche. In front of each celebrity is a cloche, underneath which is an ingredient.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie's rustling up pork steaks, Hungarian pepper sauce, and rice, plus a killer kedgeree with beans, greens, and chilli yoghurt.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Love To Cook
programme.desc: 'The more the merrier' is a motto Mary Berry lives by. What is better than cooking crowd-pleasing delights and meeting others who also adore feeding the masses?

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
programme.desc: NHS doctors Preeti and Sanjeev in Manchester are struggling to juggle their hectic work lives and the multiple meal demands of their three children.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood: A Baker's Life
programme.desc: Paul heads for Cyprus, where he lived for six years. He's reunited with friends and makes a classic ham and cheese croissant, baklava, spanakopita, and a Cypriot village bread.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris meet the King Family. Juggling work and a young daughter is not easy, so Sheena's go-to is pre-packed convenience food but she also ends up cooking two separate dishes every mealtime.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: We get exclusive access to some of the largest food factories in Britain to reveal the secrets behind food production on an epic scale. We look at Britain's love of chocolate.

programme.title: Secret World of Snacks
programme.desc: Jo Brand narrates the story of the very cold war between our beloved ice cream brands, from the early innovations of Wall's, to the sophistication of Mars, and the high-end luxury of Haagen-Dazs.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Lancashire baker Molly, hopes to make a splash with her next cake as she's asked to bake a cake replica of a very special carp- the prize catch of angler Andrew, as a surprise for his 50th birthday. Wedding cake specialist Suzanne, takes a break from the norm as she's asked to make a 30th birthday cake for a modern art fan, who wants an edible installation as a centrepiece for his party. Built from 3 spherical tiers stacked on top of each other at precarious angles, Suzanne battles to get her ganache and fondant coverings smooth enough for the high gloss finish she needs to make this cake a success.

programme.title: Secret World of Snacks
programme.desc: Jo Brand narrates the story of the very cold war between our beloved ice cream brands, from the early innovations of Wall's, to the sophistication of Mars, and the high-end luxury of Haagen-Dazs.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers get busy with batter, beating up wild waffles, an apple clafoutis and a 60-layer crepe cake.

programme.title: The Great Canadian Bake Off
programme.desc: This episode is Bread Week and the bakers must create a focaccia for the Signature Challenge. In the Technical Challenge, they tackle Montreal bagels, while the Showstopper has the bakers creating a sweet bread centrepiece.

programme.title: Jeremy Pang's Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: Jeremy's dishes this week are all comfort food, starting with a meal from his childhood - Guangxi Steamed Chicken topped with chives.

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Join Justine in the French countryside, savouring mini croissants, exploring the world-famous Chablis wine at Domaine Laroche, and indulging in iconic madeleines.

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Justine relishes award-winning goat cheese at La Ferme d'Ulteria, crafts a fresh salad, and collaborates with Chef Leo for the Captain's Dinner in the historic Dordogne region.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's My Swedish Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel picks kale from the west coast of Sweden, and continues to the only large-scale tomato farm in Sweden. In her cabin, she makes a wonderful dish with butternut squash waffles and a mixed cherry tomato salad with crispy bacon.

programme.title: Legends Of The Fork
programme.desc: Buddy visits two generational legacies: family owned and operated Patsy's Italian Restaurant in New York City, and the famed Oaxacan joint in Los Angeles, Guelaguetza.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Andy is joined by Mark Lebrooy as they collaborate to create a flavoursome dish of crispy skin pork belly, expertly slow-cooked in Master stock and served with a delectable three-flavour sauce.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Michael is joined by Andy in the kitchen as they craft a delicious miso eggplant dish that tantalises the tastebuds. Next, Andy prepares a barbecue whole snapper.

programme.title: Paddock To Plate
programme.desc: Matt Moran explores the Riverland Region, hoping to snag a Murray Cod and release it back into the wild. He then heads inland to taste local almonds, with Australia now the second largest producer of almonds in the world. Organic citrus is his next discovery, followed by a hunting expedition for duck.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine With Me
programme.desc: In Essex, three rival duos battle it out to host the best dinner party and win a 1000-pounds prize.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine With Me
programme.desc: First to host are Scottish tea room owner Melanie and her Irish husband David, who've blended together both their heritages with a Celtic fusion menu.

programme.title: Jamie's One Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie makes three one-pan delights: speedy smoked salmon pasta, a loaded beef burger with peppers and pickles, and soft sticky toffee apple buns.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom cooks a tasty posh version of chicken and chips. He serves each guest their own poussin, served with wedges and two salads, ranch and panzanella.

programme.title: Jamie's Fish Supper
programme.desc: Jamie cooks his favourite fish dishes, perfect for any summer supper.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: Rick visits Berlin, where history and the avant-garde dwell enticingly side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall teems now with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: Focusing on homegrown and seasonal produce, James takes a crop of fresh British flavours and transports them into recipes to inspire gardeners and home cooks alike. He is joined by Kate Garraway.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined in Northern Ireland by his good pal and local boy Paul Rankin. They brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge, then James cooks a tasty fish dish with the dramatic coastline as his backdrop.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's American Adventure continues with a cruise along California's Pacific Coast. James rides his motorcycle through the coastal city of Santa Cruz, taking in the fertile nature of the local farmland along the way. James shops at a local farmers' market before cooking his own recipes on a family run artichoke farm. But James couldn't leave before trying his hand at surfing, only to be upstaged by a surfing dog! James also uses the area's rich farming heritage as inspiration to cook California style back at home in the UK.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: This week, the hairy bikers are in god's county, Yorkshire and more especially the Yorkshire coast. The plan is simple, riding through this beautiful location and visiting top food producers who not only have amazing products but equally amazing stories to tell.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: How Do They Really Do It
programme.desc: This episode goes inside Primark to reveal how a small Irish department store became Britain's biggest clothing retailer, doubling their number of stores in the last 15 years, while the rest of the high street struggles. We go inside to find out the secrets of their success, from the buying team in Dublin HQ, to the world's biggest Primark in Birmingham. We discover how their unique approach to advertising gets them plenty of publicity without spending on TV ads. We see how their stores are carefully designed and curated to get customers in through the door and spending freely. We hear how Primark keeps their prices lower than the competition, and find out how their make up stands up against the big brands. And we hear exactly how Primark keeps their prices so low.

programme.title: River Cottage Summer Collection
programme.desc: Intrepid chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall brings a back-to-nature attitude to modern cooking and consumption. As his mission encompasses real people with real food issues, the host takes viewers into homes around Britain to show ordinary folks how easy and enjoyable it can be to move from pre-processed foods to nutritious natural products.

programme.title: River Cottage: Summer's Here
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall returns to River Cottage HQ to explore and celebrate the very best food that summer has to offer, including a lesson in sheep sheering and veal recipes.

programme.title: Beautiful Baking With Juliet Sear
programme.desc: With Christmas fast approaching Juliet turns her attention to baking with children. She makes a festive reindeer cake, a gingerbread house cake, a candy cane cake and snowmen cakes.

programme.title: The Sopranos

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: An enraged Johnny Sack seeks vengeance after a disparaging remark is made about his wife; Silvio and Christopher make an offer to an old-time hit man; Tony and Carmela disagree about the family finances.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony discovers he has a knack for horse racing when Ralph buys a filly named Pie-O-My. Ralph wins $40,000 and shares it with Tony, whose tough-guy demeanour softens around the animal.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: When a deal goes bad, restaurateur Artie Bucco takes an overdose of pills and alcohol and places a distraught call to Tony. Tony receives some disturbing news about his ex-mistress.

programme.desc: Tony reunites with his paroled cousin, Tony Blundetto. Paulie and Christopher relive some not-so-special memories. Carmela launches a film club and Adriana grapples with her guilt and a familiar figure "crosses over".

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Carmine Lupertazzi dies without naming a successor, presenting Johnny Sack with the opportunity to seize control. Lorraine Calluzzo fails to acknowledge his new-found power, prompting him to take extreme measures.

programme.desc: Carmela and Tony reunite, if only to discuss Anthony Jr's dwindling college prospects. Feech takes Tony's crew on a trip down memory lane and Anthony Jr has a night to remember in Manhattan.

programme.desc: Tony faces the possibility that he may have skin cancer and must deal with his feelings for Adriana. While Christopher is away on business the Sopranos crew spreads a dangerous rumour.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony B is making an effort to go straight but is derailed, AJ is failing his English class and Carmela visits his guidance counsellor who persuades her to go to dinner with him.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony meets Johnny Soprano's long-time mistress at his grave site; Christopher takes a former rehab friend to the Executive Game, where he amasses a huge debt.

programme.desc: A rumour involving Tony B jeopardises Tony's relationship with Johnny Sack. Meadow helps her boyfriend get a job while Melfi links Tony's current guilt to a past job.

programme.desc: Tony reunites with his paroled cousin, Tony Blundetto. Paulie and Christopher relive some not-so-special memories. Carmela launches a film club and Adriana grapples with her guilt and a familiar figure "crosses over".

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Carmine Lupertazzi dies without naming a successor, presenting Johnny Sack with the opportunity to seize control. Lorraine Calluzzo fails to acknowledge his new-found power, prompting him to take extreme measures.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: Flight attendant Cassandra Bowden wakes up in the wrong hotel, in the wrong bed, with a dead man, and no idea what happened.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: Rattled by her own lies and under pressure from the FBI, Cassie goes against Annie's advice to lay low and tries to find some answers by digging into Alex Sokolov's work life with bizarre and shocking results.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: Returning from Rome to find someone has broken into her apartment, Cassie doubles down on finding Miranda, even if it means crashing Alex Sokolov's champagne-soaked memorial service at his family home.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: After piecing together evidence from Alex's family home, Cassie follows the paper trail to a private jet service. But she also must juggle a visit from her brother Davey.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: With Annie's job on the line and the FBI closing in, Cassie enlists Max in a plan that involves breaking and entering, computer fraud, too much vodka, and a flash drive.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: Reeling from a major fallout with Annie, and running from anything related to Alex, Cassie brings Buckley along for an alcohol-fuelled escapade that ends in a meltdown.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: Cassie is forced to work with a surprising new ally as she reconsiders all that's happened since Bangkok; Megan's life begins to implode when her husband faces consequences for her actions.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: After the shocking reveal of Alex's killer, Cassie and a new ally devise a plan to fix everything that unfolds disastrously in Rome. Cassie, finally clear-eyed, won't let things go south.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: Cassie is living her best sober life while moonlighting as a CIA asset, but when an assignment leads her to witness a murder, she becomes entangled in another international intrigue.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.sub-title: Mushrooms, Tasers, And Bears, Oh My!
programme.desc: Cassie reveals her secret to Annie and Max and sets out to find her mysterious impersonator, who she suspects is the new flight attendant, Grace. Annie prepares for an interview.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.sub-title: The Reykjavik Ice Sculpture Festival Is Lovely This Time Of Year
programme.desc: A cryptic text leads Cassie to search for Megan in Reykjavik, where Shane grows increasingly suspicious of his friend's erratic travels.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: After attempting to get Megan safely out of Reykjavik, Cassie fears she's being framed for murder when two bodies are found in Echo Park.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: After finding potentially incriminating photos in Benjamin's office, Cassie seeks assurances from Shane and continues to avoid Marco, while Megan searches for an all-important lock box.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: While travelling to New York with Davey to pay their mum a visit, Cassie faces hard truths about her path to sobriety. Meanwhile, Megan attempts to reconnect with her reluctant family.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: With Shane's help, Cassie runs down a lead in the string of murders which inexplicably points to her as the main suspect.

programme.title: The Flight Attendant
programme.desc: Still in shock from the events at the Pier, Cassie manages to get even closer to connecting the dots in a far-reaching conspiracy. After arriving in LA, Megan looks to make amends with Shane.
